q: A security engineer needs to detect unauthorized API calls across all accounts in an organization. Which service?
e: Organization trail: single CloudTrail captures all API calls across all member accounts. Centralized to one S3 bucket.

q: A company needs to detect cryptocurrency mining on EC2. Which service provides this out of the box?
e: GuardDuty: ML-based threat detection. CryptoCurrency:EC2/BitcoinTool.B detects crypto mining without configuration.

q: Aggregate findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, and third-party tools into one dashboard. Which service?
e: Security Hub: central aggregation in ASFF format. Dashboards, compliance checks, cross-account visibility.

q: Monitor VPC network traffic for anomalies. Which log source?
e: VPC Flow Logs: IP traffic metadata (source, dest, ports, action). Feed to GuardDuty for anomaly detection.

q: Investigate a GuardDuty finding by visualizing related API calls, network flows, and resource relationships. Which service?
e: Detective: correlates CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, GuardDuty findings. Graph-based entity relationship visualization.

q: Alert within 5 minutes when root account is used. Best approach?
o: CloudTrail + CloudWatch Logs metric filter + SNS alarm
e: Metric filter on userIdentity.type=Root. CloudWatch alarm triggers SNS. Near real-time alerting.

q: Detect IAM access keys not rotated in 90 days. Which service?
o: AWS Config with access-keys-rotated managed rule
e: Config managed rule: evaluates keys against rotation period. Non-compliant triggers remediation.

q: Detect sensitive data in S3 across multiple accounts. Which service?
e: Macie: ML-based discovery of PII, credentials, financial data in S3. Multi-account via Organizations.

q: Query CloudTrail logs spanning 2 years for forensics. Most efficient?
o: CloudTrail Lake or Athena to query logs in S3
e: CloudTrail Lake: SQL queries on event data store. Athena: query JSON in S3. Both handle years of data.

q: Detect security group changes in real-time and auto-remediate. Which combination?
o: AWS Config rule + auto-remediation with SSM
e: Config detects drift. SSM Automation reverts unauthorized changes automatically.

q: Centralize security logs from AWS and on-premises for SIEM. Which service?
e: Security Lake: centralizes in OCSF format from AWS and third-party. Single lake for SIEM.

q: Detect EC2 communicating with command-and-control servers. Which service?
e: GuardDuty: threat intel feeds identify C2. Trojan:EC2/C&CActivity. No configuration needed.

q: Verify all S3 buckets encrypted across 50 accounts. Most scalable?
e: Config org rules: deploy across all accounts. Continuous compliance. Centralized reporting.

q: Suspected EC2 compromise. First containment action preserving evidence?
o: Change security group to deny all traffic
e: Isolate via SG: denies all traffic. Instance running, memory preserved, EBS intact for forensics.

q: Compromised IAM access keys discovered. What FIRST?
e: Disable first: stops unauthorized access immediately. Then investigate scope.

q: After isolating compromised EC2, how to capture forensic evidence?
o: Create EBS snapshots and memory dump
e: EBS snapshots: forensic copy. Memory dump via SSM. Preserve chain of custody.

q: Automated response to isolate EC2 when GuardDuty detects threat. Architecture?
e: EventBridge + Lambda: automated. GuardDuty triggers rule, Lambda isolates. Sub-minute containment.

q: IAM role used by attacker. Revoke all active sessions immediately. How?
o: Deny-all inline policy with aws:TokenIssueTime condition
e: Deny with DateLessThan on TokenIssueTime. All existing sessions immediately ineffective.

q: Determine what compromised access key did in last 24 hours. Which service?
o: CloudTrail Event History filtered by access key ID
e: CloudTrail: filter by accessKeyId. Event History shows last 90 days in console.

q: Automate containment of compromised account in organization. What?
o: SCP denying all except investigation actions, applied to quarantine OU
e: Quarantine OU with restrictive SCP. Blocks all except forensic actions. Fast containment.

q: Forensic analysis on compromised EC2 in isolated environment. Best approach?
o: Mount EBS snapshot to forensic EC2 in isolated VPC
e: Isolated forensic VPC: no internet, no lateral movement. Clean forensic instance.

q: S3 bucket with sensitive data was public for 3 days. What FIRST?
o: Remove public access, then assess exposure via CloudTrail and access logs
e: Contain: remove public access. Then investigate who changed it, who accessed data.

q: Preserve CloudTrail logs as legal evidence with integrity guarantee. How?
o: Log file integrity validation + separate account + Object Lock
e: Integrity validation proves tampering. Separate account. Object Lock: immutable WORM evidence.

q: Active DDoS on web app behind ALB. Immediate mitigation?
o: Enable Shield Advanced and engage SRT
e: Shield Advanced: DDoS protection, SRT for active attacks. WAF rate-limiting. ALB auto-scales.

q: Inspect and filter outbound VPC traffic to block malicious domains. Which service?
o: AWS Network Firewall with domain filtering
e: Network Firewall: stateful inspection, domain allow/deny lists, IDS/IPS signatures.

q: Protect CloudFront from XSS and SQLi. What to attach?
o: AWS WAF with managed rule groups
e: WAF on CloudFront: Managed Rules inspect HTTP at edge before reaching origin.

q: Manage EC2 without opening SSH ports. What to use?
e: Session Manager: no inbound ports, no keys, no bastion. IAM-based. Full audit.

q: Keep all VPC-to-AWS-service traffic on private network. What to implement?
o: VPC Endpoints (Gateway for S3/DynamoDB, Interface for others)
e: VPC Endpoints: private connectivity. Gateway (free) for S3/DynamoDB. Interface for others.

q: Third-party firewall appliance for transparent traffic inspection. Which service?
e: GWLB: transparent appliance insertion. GENEVE encapsulation. Scales with traffic.

q: Lambda must access only VPC resources, no internet. Configuration?
o: Lambda in private subnet with no NAT, no internet route
e: Private subnet, no NAT = no internet. VPC endpoints for AWS services.

q: Protect API Gateway from volumetric attacks. What combination?
e: WAF rate limiting blocks excess. API keys identify callers. Throttling per method.

q: Encrypt traffic over Direct Connect. What?
e: VPN over DX: IPsec tunnel provides encryption in transit.

q: Enforce EC2 launch only from approved AMIs. How?
o: IAM condition ec2:ImageId or Config rule approved-amis-by-id
e: IAM condition restricts launch to specific AMIs. Config detects non-compliant post-launch.

q: Microsegmentation between workloads in same VPC. What?
o: Security Groups referencing other SGs with least-privilege
e: SGs referencing other SGs: instance-level control. Allow only from specific source SGs.

q: Centrally manage WAF, Shield, SG policies across all accounts. Which service?
e: Firewall Manager: deploy WAF, Shield, SG policies across all Organization accounts.

q: Signature-based network intrusion detection/prevention at VPC level. Which service?
o: Network Firewall with Suricata IPS rules
e: Network Firewall: Suricata-compatible IDS/IPS. Block attack signatures at network level.

q: Encrypt all inter-instance traffic within VPC. Best approach?
o: Nitro instances encrypt between instances by default
e: Nitro: automatic encryption at network layer between instances. No app changes.

q: Restrict outbound to only approved domains. Which service?
e: Network Firewall domain filtering: allow only approved FQDNs. Block all other outbound.

q: Implement least-privilege for new team. Recommended approach?
o: Start with managed policies, use Access Analyzer to scope down
e: Access Analyzer generates policies from CloudTrail activity. Start broader, scope to actual usage.

q: Cross-account S3 access without long-term credentials. Most secure?
o: IAM role with trust policy for other account
e: Cross-account role: trust policy specifies source account. External ID for third parties. Temp creds via STS.

q: All IAM users must have MFA, cannot act without it. How?
o: IAM deny all except self-management when MultiFactorAuthPresent is false
e: Deny with MFA condition: blocks all unless MFA authenticated. Allows only MFA setup.

q: Prevent accounts from leaving Organization. What?
e: SCP deny LeaveOrganization at root/OU. No member can override even with admin.

q: Federate AD with AWS SSO across 200 accounts. Best?
o: IAM Identity Center with AD Connector
e: Identity Center: centralized SSO, integrates AD, permission sets. Single sign-on to all.

q: Policy grants s3:* on *. How to scope down?
o: Access Analyzer policy generation from CloudTrail
e: Access Analyzer: analyzes actual actions in CloudTrail. Generates least-privilege policy.

q: Lambda role cannot be assumed outside Lambda. What to add?
o: Trust policy: only lambda.amazonaws.com with SourceAccount condition
e: Trust policy with lambda.amazonaws.com principal. SourceAccount prevents cross-account confusion.

q: Break-glass elevated access with full audit. Approach?
o: Time-limited STS role assumption with session tags and approval workflow
e: Time-limited STS with session tags. CloudTrail logs all. Approval workflow gates access.

q: Restrict actions to specific Regions only. What?
e: SCP region condition: deny where Region not approved. Exceptions for global services.

q: Identify unused IAM roles and permissions across accounts. Tool?
o: IAM Access Analyzer unused access findings
e: Access Analyzer: identifies unused roles, unexercised permissions. Recommends removal.

q: S3 API calls from role must come from within VPC only. Condition?
o: aws:SourceVpce or aws:SourceVpc in bucket policy
e: SourceVpce/SourceVpc restricts to traffic from specific VPC endpoint or VPC.

q: Map AD groups to different IAM roles via SAML. How?
o: SAML Role attribute maps AD groups to role ARNs
e: SAML Role attribute: role+provider ARN pairs. AD group determines which roles in assertion.

q: Prevent deletion of critical IAM roles organization-wide. What?
o: SCP denying DeleteRole/DeletePolicy for specific ARNs
e: SCP deny deletion: protects critical roles. Even admins cannot delete.

q: Enforce IMDSv2 only, disable IMDSv1. How?
e: MetadataHttpTokens condition: deny RunInstances unless tokens required. Prevents SSRF credential theft.

q: Fargate task needs cross-account access. Best?
o: Task role with sts:AssumeRole for target account
e: Task role: temp creds. Cross-account assume role. No long-term credentials.

q: Encrypt RDS with keys you control and audit. Which?
e: KMS CMK: control key policy, rotation, access. CloudTrail logs all usage.

q: Enforce TLS 1.2 minimum on ALB. Where?
o: ALB security policy (TLS-1-2 or TLS13 policy)
e: ALB security policy: defines TLS versions/ciphers per HTTPS listener.

q: DynamoDB encryption with auto-rotation and audit. What?
e: Customer-managed KMS: yearly rotation, CloudTrail audit, granular key policy access control.

q: Deny S3 uploads not using specific KMS key. How?
o: Bucket policy deny PutObject unless kms-key-id matches
e: Bucket policy condition: deny if key ARN mismatch. Enforces specific key for all uploads.

q: Share encrypted EBS snapshots cross-account. What must be done?
o: Modify KMS key policy to grant target account, then share
e: Grant target kms:Decrypt and CreateGrant in key policy. Target copies and re-encrypts.

q: Prevent KMS key deletion. Controls?
o: Deletion waiting period + SCP deny ScheduleKeyDeletion
e: Waiting period 7-30 days. SCP deny prevents accidental deletion organization-wide.

q: End-to-end encryption where AWS cannot see plaintext. What?
e: Client-side: encrypted before reaching AWS. AWS never sees plaintext. Customer manages keys.

q: CloudHSM survive AZ failure. Architecture?
e: Cluster across AZs: auto-sync. AZ failure doesn't lose keys. HA for crypto.

q: Encrypt transit between ECS microservices. What?
e: mTLS: both sides authenticate. ACM Private CA issues certs. App Mesh automates.

q: Restrict KMS key to specific services and accounts. Where?
o: Key policy with kms:ViaService and kms:CallerAccount
e: Key policy: ViaService restricts to services. CallerAccount limits to accounts.

q: Mask SSN/credit card in CloudWatch Logs. What?
e: Data protection policy: auto-detects and masks PII/PHI/financial with managed identifiers.

q: Private TLS certificates for internal services. Which?
e: ACM Private CA: private certs for internal resources. Full lifecycle management.

q: Multi-account setup. Recommended structure?
o: Organizations with OUs (prod, dev, security, shared)
e: Organizations + OUs: Security OU, workload OUs. SCPs per OU for guardrails.

q: New accounts auto-enable CloudTrail, Config, GuardDuty. What?
e: Control Tower: account factory. Mandatory guardrails. Landing zone best practices.

q: Prevent IAM user creation in member accounts (federation only). What?
e: SCP deny: enforces federation only. All access via Identity Center or SAML.

q: Deploy security baselines across 100 accounts consistently. Service?
e: StackSets: deploy across all accounts/regions. Auto-deploy to new accounts.

q: Dedicated log archive no one can modify. Best practice?
o: Log archive account in Security OU + SCPs + Object Lock
e: Isolated log archive: SCP prevents modification. Object Lock WORM. Security team only.

q: Evaluate against CIS Foundations Benchmark. Service?
o: Security Hub with CIS standard enabled
e: Security Hub: built-in CIS, PCI DSS, NIST. Automated checks with scoring.

q: Prevent CloudTrail modification even by member admins. How?
o: Organization trail + SCP deny CloudTrail modifications in members
e: Org trail centrally managed. SCP deny Stop/Update/Delete in members. Tamper-proof.

q: Enforce required tags on resource creation. How?
o: SCP/IAM with RequestTag and TagKeys conditions
e: Deny creation if required tags missing. Proactive enforcement via conditions.

q: Assess EC2 for CVEs and network exposure. Service?
e: Inspector: automated vulnerability and network reachability scanning. Continuous.

q: Restrict which services can be used in member accounts. What?
o: SCP allowlist: deny all except approved namespaces
e: SCP allowlist with NotAction: only whitelisted services usable. Strictest governance.

q: KMS separation of duties: admins cannot decrypt. How?
o: Separate key admins from key users via key policy
e: Key policy: admins manage (enable/disable/rotate). Users encrypt/decrypt. Separation.

q: Detect config drift from security baseline. Service?
e: Conformance packs: rule collections for compliance. Continuous evaluation, drift detection.
